item.title: A message from Prom Committee:
item.description: The Cake Auction & Bake Sale is happening TOMORROW during high school lunch in the atrium! Come hungry, bring your friends, and get ready to bid on amazing cakes and grab delicious treats!

item.title: A message from Ms. Montgomery:
item.description: Reminder to all players interested in the try outs for the Varsity boys baseball team to sign up by Friday. Sign up outside my office in the library and return your signed participation form ASAP.

item.title: A message from Ms. Clouthier.
item.description: Congratulations to our Grade 7 and 8 boys basketball team on an incredible tournament on Monday! The boys made it to the finals and came up just a few points short in a very close and well-played game. Throughout their games, the team showed amazing effort, sportsmanship, teamwork, integrity, and a positive attitude. Even when faced with unexpected rules and different defenses from other teams, they stayed focused, worked hard, and supported one another. You should be extremely proud yourselves! They represented STA with pride and truly made our school proud.

item.title: Another message from Ms. Clouthier.
item.description: There will be a brief 7&8 boys basketball meeting in room 244 at the beginning of the morning break (9:05) today to discuss the games tomorrow.

item.title: A message from Mr. Cornellier.
item.description: Girls who are interested in trying out for the slo-pitch team are asked to sign their names on the sheet located just outside the gym doors. First tryout in on April 22nd. Don't forget to pick up your permission form.

item.title: A message from: Mrs. Longval
item.description: Awesome 1st day for our Lunchtime Volleyball Tournament! Today is Day 2 - all teams are reminded to arrive early to warm up! Good luck!

item.title: A message from: Mr. Benoit and Mr. McLaren:
item.description: Congratulations to the Tier 1 Grade 7 & 8 hockey team on capturing the St. Michael’s Cup yesterday in Kemptville! The team delivered an outstanding performance, winning all three games with scores of 6–2, 7–1, and a 5–3 victory in the finals. They showcased strong offensive plays, solid defensive effort, and excellent goaltending throughout the tournament. Way to go, Ravens!
